edges.description: Linker-enzyme defects reduce mature proteoglycan and sulfated glycosaminoglycan abundance, disrupting cartilage extracellular matrix organization.
edges.target: Proteoglycan depletion and cartilage matrix disorganization

edges.description: B3GALT6 linker dysfunction is measurable as abnormal urinary bikunin glycosaminoglycan linkage-region structures.
B4GALT7-related glycosaminoglycan biosynthesis defects are associated with delayed motor and cognitive development, although the neurodevelopmental intermediates remain unresolved.
Proteoglycan and cartilage-matrix disruption contributes to the short stature that defines the spondylodysplastic phenotype.
edges.source: Proteoglycan depletion and cartilage matrix disorganization

edges.description: Impaired cartilage and skeletal patterning can manifest as bowing of long bones or analogous limb skeletal abnormalities.
edges.source: Proteoglycan depletion and cartilage matrix disorganization

edges.description: B4GALT7-related matrix and skeletal patterning defects are associated with radioulnar synostosis.
edges.source: Proteoglycan depletion and cartilage matrix disorganization

edges.description: Disturbed proteoglycan-rich bone and cartilage matrix contributes to low bone mineral density in some spEDS patients.
edges.source: Proteoglycan depletion and cartilage matrix disorganization

edges.description: Matrix laxity manifests as hyperextensible or fragile skin.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Peripheral joint connective-tissue weakness manifests as joint hypermobility.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Ligamentous and connective-tissue laxity contributes to flat feet.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Matrix fragility in acral skin manifests as thin, finely wrinkled skin.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility
edges.target: Thin Wrinkled Skin of Hands and Feet

edges.description: Ocular connective-tissue thinning manifests as bluish sclerae.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Corneal connective-tissue weakness can manifest as keratoconus.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Ocular connective-tissue involvement in SLC39A13-spEDS includes myopia, potentially related to thin sclerae.
edges.source: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: ER/Golgi iron deficiency deprives lysyl and prolyl hydroxylases of their Fe2+ cofactor, reducing collagen hydroxylation.
edges.source: ER/Golgi Iron Deficiency Due to ZIP13 Loss

edges.description: Reduced collagen hydroxylation leads to defective crosslinking, as measured by a reduced urinary pyridinoline-to-deoxypyridinoline ratio.
edges.source: ER/Golgi Iron Deficiency Due to ZIP13 Loss

edges.description: ZIP13 is involved in myogenic differentiation; its loss impairs myotube formation, contributing to the muscular hypotonia observed in SLC39A13-spEDS.
edges.source: ER/Golgi Iron Deficiency Due to ZIP13 Loss

edges.description: SLC39A13 loss is associated with a characteristic craniofacial, dental, ocular, and short-stature presentation; intermediates beyond collagen hydroxylation remain incompletely resolved.
edges.source: ER/Golgi Iron Deficiency Due to ZIP13 Loss

edges.description: Reduced lysyl hydroxylation produces abnormal collagen-derived crosslink ratios.
Reduced collagen hydroxylation is captured clinically by a low urinary pyridinoline-to-deoxypyridinoline ratio.
Defective collagen crosslinking is measured as a reduced urinary pyridinoline-to-deoxypyridinoline ratio.
Crosslinking defects weaken collagen-rich connective tissues.
edges.target: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

edges.description: Vascular connective-tissue fragility may contribute to cerebrovascular events in SLC39A13-spEDS, but the causal status remains uncertain.
Connective-tissue fragility can involve venous tissues, producing varicose veins in SLC39A13-spEDS.
Collagen crosslinking defects in skeletal matrix contribute to vertebral flattening in SLC39A13-related spEDS.
Incomplete myogenic differentiation provides a mechanistic explanation for muscular hypotonia in SLC39A13-spEDS.
SLC39A13-related tissue-development dysplasia includes postnatal short stature.
Craniofacial dysplasia manifests with downslanting palpebral fissures.
Craniofacial and periorbital connective-tissue changes manifest as prominent eyes.
Craniofacial dysplasia manifests as mild hypertelorism.
Dental development involvement manifests as oligodontia.

evidence.reference_title: Glycosaminoglycan linkage region of urinary bikunin as a potentially useful biomarker for beta3GalT6-deficient spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: contained two different glycopeptide peaks, corresponding to the canonical tetrasaccharide and to the non-canonical trisaccharide linkage region modifications

nodes.description: Blue discoloration of the sclerae, reflecting thin scleral connective tissue, is characteristic of SLC39A13-spEDS.

evidence.reference_title: The Connective Tissue Disorder Associated with Recessive Variants in the SLC39A13 Zinc Transporter Gene (Spondylo-Dysplastic Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome Type 3): Insights from Four Novel Patients and Follow-Up on Two Original Cases.
evidence.snippet: thin and finely wrinkled skin of the hands and feet, characteristic facial features with downslanting palpebral fissures, mild hypertelorism, prominent eyes with a paucity of periorbital fat, blueish sclerae

nodes.description: Bowing of long bones is a radiographic finding reported in spEDS. Zebrafish models of B4GALT7 deficiency recapitulate limb bowing phenotypes.

evidence.reference_title: Hypomorphic zebrafish models mimic the musculoskeletal phenotype of β4GalT7-deficient Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Morphant and crispant zebrafish show highly similar morphological abnormalities in early development including a small, round head, bowed pectoral fins, short body-axis and mild developmental delay

nodes.description: Cerebrovascular accidents, including cerebral hemorrhage or ischemic events, have been reported in older SLC39A13-spEDS patients. The causal relationship remains uncertain, but the finding is noted as a potential vascular complication.

evidence.reference_title: The Connective Tissue Disorder Associated with Recessive Variants in the SLC39A13 Zinc Transporter Gene (Spondylo-Dysplastic Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome Type 3): Insights from Four Novel Patients and Follow-Up on Two Original Cases.
evidence.snippet: Two of our patients developed severe keratoconus, and two suffered from cerebrovascular accidents in their twenties, suggesting that there may be a vascular component to this condition.

evidence.reference_title: The Connective Tissue Disorder Associated with Recessive Variants in the SLC39A13 Zinc Transporter Gene (Spondylo-Dysplastic Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome Type 3): Insights from Four Novel Patients and Follow-Up on Two Original Cases.
evidence.snippet: We describe here four additional affected individuals from three consanguineous families and the follow up of two of the original cases.

nodes.description: Abnormal proteoglycan abundance and collagen modification weaken the extracellular matrix of skin, ligaments, sclerae, cornea, and peripheral joints, producing connective-tissue laxity and fragility.
nodes.id: Connective tissue matrix laxity and skin fragility

evidence.snippet: connective tissue weakness affecting mainly the skin and peripheral joints, a characteristic facial appearance, and a moderate skeletal dysplasia

evidence.reference_title: Report of two siblings with spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and B4GALT7 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: short stature and generalized muscular hypotonia as major criteria, and skin hyperextensibility, soft doughy skin, pes planus, delayed motor and cognitive development and osteopenia as minor criteria

nodes.description: Reduced collagen hydroxylation alters collagen-derived crosslink chemistry, weakening connective tissues and contributing to skeletal, skin, ocular, and joint manifestations in SLC39A13-related spEDS.

evidence.reference_title: The Connective Tissue Disorder Associated with Recessive Variants in the SLC39A13 Zinc Transporter Gene (Spondylo-Dysplastic Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome Type 3): Insights from Four Novel Patients and Follow-Up on Two Original Cases.
evidence.snippet: The observation of reduced collagen hydroxylation indicates that a partial failure of collagen crosslinking is one pathogenic mechanism

nodes.description: B4GALT7 and B3GALT6 encode enzymes required for the common tetrasaccharide linker region that initiates glycosaminoglycan chain assembly on proteoglycan core proteins. Loss of function leads to insufficient mature proteoglycans, disrupting extracellular matrix structure in bone, cartilage, and connective tissues.

evidence.reference_title: Report of two siblings with spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and B4GALT7 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: galactosyltransferase I, which is required for the initiation of glycosaminoglycan side chain synthesis of proteoglycans

evidence.reference_title: Ehlers Danlos Syndrome with Glycosaminoglycan Abnormalities.
evidence.snippet: Spondylodysplastic EDS is caused by pathogenic variants in B4GALT7 or B3GALT6, both of which encode key enzymes that initiate glycosaminoglycan synthesis

evidence.reference_title: Glycosaminoglycan linkage region of urinary bikunin as a potentially useful biomarker for beta3GalT6-deficient spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: contained two different glycopeptide peaks, corresponding to the canonical tetrasaccharide and to the non-canonical trisaccharide linkage region modifications

nodes.description: ZIP13 loss impairs myotube and myogenic differentiation in cellular models and patient-derived iPSC myocytes, providing a mechanistic route to muscular hypotonia.

evidence.reference_title: Possible involvement of zinc transporter ZIP13 in myogenic differentiation.
evidence.snippet: We found that ZIP13 gene expression was upregulated by myogenic stimulation in C2C12 cells, and its knockdown disrupted myotubular differentiation

evidence.reference_title: Possible involvement of zinc transporter ZIP13 in myogenic differentiation.
evidence.snippet: Myocytes differentiated from iPSCs derived from patients with EDSSPD3 (EDSSPD3-iPSCs) also exhibited incomplete myogenic differentiation

nodes.description: Delayed motor and cognitive development is reported as a minor diagnostic criterion and possible hallmark of B4GALT7-related spEDS.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the clinical and mutational spectrum of B4GALT7-spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: in addition to short stature and muscle hypotonia, radioulnar synostosis, hypermetropia, and delayed cognitive development might be a hallmark of this specific type of spEDS

evidence.reference_title: Report of two siblings with spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome and B4GALT7 deficiency.
evidence.snippet: skin hyperextensibility, soft doughy skin, pes planus, delayed motor and cognitive development and osteopenia as minor criteria

nodes.description: Downslanting palpebral fissures are part of the characteristic facial gestalt in SLC39A13-spEDS.

evidence.reference_title: The Connective Tissue Disorder Associated with Recessive Variants in the SLC39A13 Zinc Transporter Gene (Spondylo-Dysplastic Ehlers-Danlos Syndrome Type 3): Insights from Four Novel Patients and Follow-Up on Two Original Cases.
evidence.snippet: characteristic facial features with downslanting palpebral fissures, mild hypertelorism, prominent eyes with a paucity of periorbital fat, blueish sclerae

nodes.description: SLC39A13 encodes the transporter ZIP13, historically classified as a zinc transporter. A 2024 study demonstrated that ZIP13's primary physiological function is iron transport into the ER/Golgi. ZIP13 loss causes iron deficiency in the ER/Golgi compartment, depriving Fe2+-dependent enzymes of their required cofactor.
nodes.id: ER/Golgi Iron Deficiency Due to ZIP13 Loss

evidence.reference_title: Mammalian SLC39A13 promotes ER/Golgi iron transport and iron homeostasis in multiple compartments.
evidence.snippet: ZIP13 loss causes an iron deficiency in the ER/Golgi and other intracellular compartments, such as lysosomes and mitochondria, as well as elevating iron in the cytosol

nodes.description: Skin hyperextensibility is a minor criterion for spEDS. In SLC39A13-related cases, the skin is characteristically thin and finely wrinkled on the hands and feet.

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the clinical and mutational spectrum of B4GALT7-spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.
evidence.snippet: The majority of spEDS patients presented with short stature, skin hyperextensibility, facial dysmorphisms, peculiar radiological findings, muscle hypotonia and joint laxity

nodes.description: Mild hypertelorism is part of the characteristic facial features in SLC39A13-spEDS.

nodes.description: Generalized or distal joint hypermobility is present across subtypes. In SLC39A13-spEDS it is most marked in finger joints. A systematic review found joint hypermobility in 96% of spEDS cases (24/25).

evidence.reference_title: Extracutaneous features and complications of the Ehlers-Danlos syndromes: A systematic review.
evidence.snippet: We noted a high prevalence of joint hypermobility amongst kyphoscoliotic (39/39, 100%), spondylodysplastic (24/25, 96.0%), and hypermobile (153/160, 95.6%) EDS subtypes

evidence.reference_title: Expanding the clinical and mutational spectrum of B4GALT7-spondylodysplastic Ehlers-Danlos syndrome.

nodes.description: SLC39A13-related spEDS includes a recurrent craniofacial, ocular, dental, and short-stature pattern. The clinical data indicate that some of these features are not fully explained by collagen hydroxylation alone, so this node records the tissue-development branch with unresolved intermediates.
